title: Fill Loot
description: Efficiently fill thousands of containers in under a second with any loot table you can think of!
body:
<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/n192YalHvsI?si=BBeXeWeBr8Oh4S1i&start=1" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

# Flexible Loot Sources
- 56 Vanilla Loot Tables: All Minecraft loot tables including Trial Chambers, Ancient Cities, Villages, Bastions, and more
- Custom Loot Tables: Create your own loot tables with precise control over items, chances, and quantities
- Random Loot: Generate random weighted loot for variety
# User-Friendly Interface
- Interactive GUI: Browse and manage containers with an intuitive menu
- Pagination: Navigate through large lists easily
- Filtering & Sorting: Filter by empty/filled, sort by distance or material
- Visual Progress: Real-time progress updates with TPS monitoring
# Advanced Controls
- Selective Filling: Choose to skip already-filled containers
- Bulk Operations: Fill or clear hundreds of containers at once
- Container Detection: Automatically finds all containers within radius
- Rollback Prevention: Skip filled containers to prevent accidental overwrites

# Vanilla Loot Tables Included
- Villages _(15)_: All biome variants, profession shops, temples
- Ocean _(6)_: Shipwrecks, underwater ruins, buried treasure
- Nether _(6)_: All bastion types, nether fortress, ruined portal
- Trial Chambers _(6)_: Corridor, intersection, entrance, supply, chamber, reward
- Ancient Cities _(2)_: Main chest, ice box
- Strongholds _(3)_: Corridor, crossing, library
- End Cities _(1)_: Treasure
- And more! Desert pyramids, jungle temples, igloos, dungeons, etc.
